active_question: What is the supplied link for the X-POLE A-FRAME?
edited_answer: The page is https://xpoleus.com/shop-all/aerial/a-frame/xpole-a-frame/. The manual gives heights of 2,255–3,480 millimetres (89–137 inches, about 7.5–11.42 feet) and corresponding floor areas of 3.6–8.9 square metres (38–95 square feet).
edited_question: Where is the owner-recommended X-POLE A-FRAME page, and what height and floor-area ranges does its manual specify?
paraphrase_rationale: The original answer preserved only the product link. The replacement keeps that exact owner-recommended URL and adds the official manual's height and floor-area ranges for practical site planning.
reason: The original answer preserved only the product link. The replacement keeps that exact owner-recommended URL and adds the official manual's height and floor-area ranges for practical site planning.
support_evidence:
The supplied directory recommends the X-POLE A-FRAME as a semi-permanent suspension-frame resource and gives this product page: https://xpoleus.com/shop-all/aerial/a-frame/xpole-a-frame/.
The official manual lists a selectable height range from 2,255 to 3,480 millimetres (89 to 137 inches, or about 7.5 to 11.42 feet). At those endpoints, the manual lists required floor areas of 3.6 and 8.9 square metres (38 and 95 square feet), respectively.
